description: Facts about disease mechanisms, molecular targets, and pathophysiology for therapeutic design

facts.context: In Cystic Fibrosis, the primary molecular defect is:
facts.truth: loss of CFTR chloride channel function causing thick mucus
distractors: overactive CFTR sodium channel causing dehydration
CFTR gain-of-function causing excess mucus secretion
mitochondrial CFTR dysfunction causing energy depletion

facts.context: BCR-ABL fusion protein causes CML through:
facts.truth: constitutive tyrosine kinase activity driving uncontrolled proliferation
distractors: constitutive phosphatase activity blocking apoptosis signals
loss of kinase activity allowing checkpoint escape
receptor internalization and lysosomal degradation

facts.context: HER2-positive breast cancer is driven by:
facts.truth: HER2 receptor overexpression causing excessive growth signaling
distractors: HER2 underexpression causing loss of contact inhibition
HER2 mutation causing constitutive nuclear translocation
HER2 truncation causing dominant-negative receptor interference

facts.truth: polyglutamine expansion in huntingtin causing toxic protein aggregation
distractors: polyalanine expansion causing protein mislocalization to cytoplasm
polyglutamine contraction causing accelerated protein degradation
huntingtin loss-of-function causing synaptic vesicle depletion

facts.truth: autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells stopping insulin production
distractors: insulin resistance in peripheral tissues despite normal production
glucagon hypersecretion from alpha cells overwhelming insulin
autoimmune destruction of pancreatic alpha cells

facts.context: KRAS G12C mutation drives cancer by:
facts.truth: locking the protein in GTP-bound active state causing constitutive signaling
distractors: locking the protein in GDP-bound inactive state blocking apoptosis
enhancing GTPase activity promoting rapid cycling
causing membrane dissociation preventing all signal transduction

facts.context: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is caused by:
facts.truth: dystrophin loss destabilizing the sarcolemma during muscle contraction
distractors: dystrophin overexpression causing fiber hypertrophy
dystrophin gain-of-function causing uncontrolled calcium influx
utrophin loss causing neuromuscular junction failure

facts.truth: LDLR loss-of-function reducing LDL clearance from blood
distractors: LDLR gain-of-function increasing endogenous cholesterol synthesis
PCSK9 loss-of-function accelerating receptor degradation
ApoB overexpression saturating clearance receptors

facts.context: Sickle cell disease pathology arises from:
facts.truth: hemoglobin S polymerization under low oxygen causing cell sickling
distractors: hemoglobin S degradation under normal oxygen conditions
hemoglobin S overproduction causing erythrocyte lysis
fetal hemoglobin loss causing oxygen delivery failure

facts.truth: alpha-synuclein aggregation forming Lewy bodies in dopaminergic neurons
distractors: alpha-synuclein degradation causing dopamine hypersecretion
tau aggregation causing cholinergic neuron death
alpha-synuclein activation causing glutamate excitotoxicity

facts.context: Hemophilia A bleeding disorder is caused by:
facts.truth: Factor VIII deficiency preventing proper clot formation
distractors: Factor VIII overactivity causing excessive clot formation
Factor IX deficiency preventing platelet aggregation
von Willebrand factor deficiency blocking Factor VIII binding

facts.context: Rheumatoid arthritis joint destruction is driven by:
facts.truth: TNF-alpha overexpression causing chronic synovial inflammation
distractors: TNF-alpha deficiency causing cartilage overgrowth
IL-10 overexpression driving bone resorption
TGF-beta activation promoting fibrotic joint fusion

facts.context: Spinal muscular atrophy motor neuron loss results from:
facts.truth: SMN1 loss reducing survival motor neuron protein levels
distractors: SMN2 overexpression causing motor neuron hyperactivity
SMN1 gain-of-function causing axonal overgrowth
dystrophin loss causing neuromuscular junction failure

facts.context: Wet age-related macular degeneration is caused by:
facts.truth: VEGF overexpression driving abnormal choroidal vessel growth
distractors: VEGF deficiency causing retinal atrophy
PDGF overexpression causing photoreceptor death
complement deficiency blocking drusen clearance

facts.truth: autoimmune demyelination of central nervous system axons
distractors: autoimmune hypermyelination of peripheral nervous system axons
infectious demyelination of autonomic nervous system fibers
metabolic demyelination of cerebellar Purkinje cell dendrites

facts.context: Gaucher disease storage disorder results from:
distractors: glucocerebrosidase overactivity causing lipid depletion
sphingomyelinase deficiency causing cholesterol accumulation
hexosaminidase deficiency causing ganglioside accumulation
